docs.title: OpenSCAP
docs.text:
OpenSCAP is an open source security compliance scanner for Linux systems.

It is used to check systems against security standards:

  • CIS Benchmarks
  • DISA STIG
  • PCI DSS
  • NIST security profiles

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Install OpenSCAP on RHEL 
sudo dnf install openscap-scanner scap-security-guide
Verify installation 
oscap --version

docs.title: Security Content
docs.text:
OpenSCAP uses security content provided by SCAP Security Guide.

The package contains profiles for:

  • CIS
  • STIG
  • PCI DSS
  • Standard security baseline
Find available security content 
ls /usr/share/xml/scap/ssg/content/
Show available profiles 
oscap info /usr/share/xml/scap/ssg/content/ssg-rhel9-ds.xml

docs.title: Basic Scan
docs.text:
Run compliance scan 
sudo oscap xccdf eval \
--profile xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_profile_standard \
--report report.html \
/usr/share/xml/scap/ssg/content/ssg-rhel9-ds.xml

The result is an HTML report:

report.html

docs.title: Remediation
docs.text:
OpenSCAP can create remediation instructions. Ansible is commonly used to apply the fixes.

Generate Ansible remediation 
oscap xccdf generate fix \
--fix-type ansible \
results.xml > remediation.yml
Run remediation 
ansible-playbook remediation.yml

docs.title: Common Commands
docs.text:
Show version 
oscap --version
Show profile information 
oscap info file.xml
Run evaluation 
oscap xccdf eval
